post.description:
Authenticates a user using username and password (OAuth2 password flow).

- **username**: The registered username.
- **password**: The user's password.

Returns an access token and token type upon successful authentication.
Raises HTTP 401 if authentication fails or user is inactive.

get.description:
Retrieves the details of the currently authenticated user.

Requires a valid bearer token in the Authorization header.

post.description:
Registers a new user in the system.

- **username**: Unique username for the new user.
- **email**: Unique email address for the new user.
- **password**: Password for the new user.
- **role**: Optional user role (defaults might apply).

Raises HTTP 400 if the username or email is already registered.

Logs the currently authenticated user out.

**Note:** This endpoint is primarily symbolic for client-side token handling.
True JWT logout requires the client application to discard the stored token.
Server-side token revocation is not implemented by default but can be added
if using refresh tokens or a token blacklist.

Requires a valid bearer token.

schema.title: Response Logout Api V1 Auth Logout Post

get.description:
Retrieves the preferences for the currently authenticated user.

If preferences don't exist for a user, default preferences will be created and returned.
Requires a valid bearer token.

put.description:
Updates the preferences for the currently authenticated user.

Only provided fields in the request body will be updated.
Requires a valid bearer token.

post.description: Authenticate user and return access token.

get.description:
Retrieves a list of all registered agents and their current status.

Communicates with the Orchestrator to get the latest information.
Requires an active user session.

schema.title: Response List Agents Api V1 Agents  Get

post.description: Create a new agent in the database (superuser only).

schema.title: Response Create Agent Db Api V1 Agents  Post

get.description: Retrieve an agent by its ID.

schema.title: Response Get Agent Db Api V1 Agents  Agent Id  Get
Response Update Agent Db Api V1 Agents  Agent Id  Put

delete.description: Delete an agent by its ID (superuser only).

schema.title: Response Delete Agent Db Api V1 Agents  Agent Id  Delete

post.description: Register an agent and obtain an auth token.

get.description:
Retrieves the detailed status for a specific agent.

- **agent_name**: The name of the agent to query.

Communicates with the Orchestrator.
Requires an active user session.
Raises HTTP 404 if the agent is not found.

Retrieves the current configuration for a specific agent.

- **agent_name**: The name of the agent whose configuration is requested.

Communicates with the Orchestrator.
Requires an active user session.
Raises HTTP 404 if the agent or its configuration is not found.

put.description:
Updates the configuration for a specific agent via the Orchestrator.

- **agent_name**: The name of the agent to update.
- **config_data**: The new configuration settings.

Communicates with the Orchestrator.
Requires superuser privileges.
Raises HTTP 404 if the agent is not found.
Raises HTTP 502 if communication with the orchestrator fails.

post.description:
Sends a message or command to a specific agent via the Orchestrator.

- **agent_name**: The target agent's name.
- **payload**: Contains the message and optional context.

Includes user information as the originator.
Requires an active user session.
Raises HTTP 404 if the agent is not found.
Raises HTTP 502 if communication with the orchestrator fails.

Triggers a self-assessment process for a specific agent.

- **agent_name**: The name of the agent to assess.

Communicates with the Orchestrator to initiate the assessment.
Requires superuser privileges.
Raises HTTP 404 if the agent is not found.
Raises HTTP 502 if the orchestrator fails to trigger the assessment.

Sends a command to the Orchestrator to start a specific agent.

- **agent_name**: The name of the agent to start.

Requires superuser privileges.
Raises HTTP 404 if the agent is not found.
Raises HTTP 502 if the orchestrator fails to start the agent.

Sends a command to the Orchestrator to stop a specific agent.

- **agent_name**: The name of the agent to stop.

Requires superuser privileges.
Raises HTTP 404 if the agent is not found.
Raises HTTP 502 if the orchestrator fails to stop the agent.

Sends a command to the Orchestrator to restart a specific agent.

- **agent_name**: The name of the agent to restart.

Requires superuser privileges.
Raises HTTP 404 if the agent is not found.
Raises HTTP 502 if the orchestrator fails to restart the agent.

Triggers a reload of all agent configurations via the Orchestrator.

This typically involves the Orchestrator re-reading configuration files.
Requires superuser privileges.
Raises HTTP 502 if the orchestrator fails to reload configurations.

get.description: Return mapping of agents to their capability methods.

schema.title: Response List Agent Capabilities Api V1 Agents Capabilities Get

get.description:
Retrieves the current overall system status from the Legion Orchestrator.

This typically includes orchestrator status and a summary of agent health.
Requires an active user session.

schema.title: Response Get System Status Api V1 System Status Get

get.description:
Retrieves system performance and operational metrics from the Orchestrator.

The specific metrics returned are determined by the Orchestrator's implementation.
Requires an active user session.

schema.title: Response Get System Metrics Api V1 System Metrics Get

get.description:
Retrieves recent system logs from the Legion Orchestrator.

Future enhancements may include filtering options.
Requires an active user session.

schema.title: Response Get System Logs Api V1 System Logs Get

get.description:
Retrieves usage statistics for the Legion memory system via the Orchestrator.

Requires an active user session.

schema.title: Response Get Memory Stats Api V1 System Memory Stats Get

get.description:
Retrieves a list of tasks from the Orchestrator, with optional filtering and pagination.

- **skip**: Number of initial tasks to skip (for pagination).
- **limit**: Maximum number of tasks per page.
- **agent**: Filter by agent name.
- **task_status**: Filter by task status.

Requires an active user session.
Raises HTTP 502 if communication with the orchestrator fails.

parameters.description: Maximum number of tasks to return.

schema.title: Limit
schema.description: Maximum number of tasks to return.

parameters.name: limit

parameters.description: Filter tasks by assigned agent name.

schema.title: Agent
schema.description: Filter tasks by assigned agent name.

parameters.name: agent

parameters.description: Filter tasks by status (e.g., pending, completed, failed).

schema.title: Task Status
schema.description: Filter tasks by status (e.g., pending, completed, failed).

parameters.name: task_status

schema.title: Response Read Tasks Api V1 Tasks  Get

post.description:
Submits a new task to the Legion Orchestrator for processing.

- **task_in**: Details of the task to be created (title, description, assigned agent).

Returns the newly created task details, including its assigned ID.
Requires an active user session.
Raises HTTP 502 if communication with the orchestrator fails.

get.description:
Retrieves the details and current status of a specific task by its UUID.

- **task_id**: The UUID of the task.

Requires an active user session.
Raises HTTP 404 if the task is not found.

parameters.description: The UUID of the task to retrieve.

schema.title: Task Id
schema.description: The UUID of the task to retrieve.

parameters.name: task_id

delete.description:
Requests the cancellation of an existing task by its UUID.

- **task_id**: The UUID of the task to cancel.

Note: Cancellation might not be immediate depending on the task state and Orchestrator logic.
Requires an active user session.
Returns HTTP 204 No Content on successful cancellation request.
Raises HTTP 502 if the cancellation request fails in the orchestrator.

parameters.description: The UUID of the task to cancel.

schema.title: Task Id
schema.description: The UUID of the task to cancel.

parameters.name: task_id

get.description: Return tasks filtered by optional query parameters.

schema.title: Response List Tasks Api V1 Registry Tasks  Get

post.description: Add a task to the registry.

get.description: Retrieve a single task by ID.

delete.description: Remove a task from the registry.

patch.description: Update fields of an existing task.

get.description: Return counts of tasks grouped by priority/state.

schema.title: Response Get Queue Summary Api V1 Queue Summary Get

get.description: List current and recent tasks for an agent.

schema.title: Response Get Agent Tasks Api V1 Agent  Agent Id  Tasks Get

get.description:
Retrieves a list of document names stored in the Legion Memory system.

Sends a `memory_list_documents` command to the orchestrator.
Requires an active user session.
Raises HTTP 502 if communication with the orchestrator fails.

schema.title: Response List Memory Documents Api V1 Memory Documents Get

get.description:
Retrieves a specific document (optionally a specific version) from the Legion Memory system.

- **doc_name**: Path parameter allowing slashes in the document name.
- **version**: Optional query parameter for a specific version ID.

Sends a `memory_get_document` command to the orchestrator.
Requires an active user session.
Raises HTTP 404 if the document/version is not found.
Raises HTTP 502 if communication with the orchestrator fails.

parameters.description: The full path/name of the document to retrieve.

schema.title: Doc Name
schema.description: The full path/name of the document to retrieve.

parameters.name: doc_name

parameters.description: Optional specific version of the document to retrieve.

schema.title: Version
schema.description: Optional specific version of the document to retrieve.

parameters.name: version

get.description:
Performs a semantic search within the vector memory of a specific agent.

- **agent_name**: The target agent's name.
- **query**: The search text.
- **top_k**: Maximum number of results (default 5, max 50).

Sends a `memory_search_agent` command to the orchestrator.
Requires an active user session.
Raises HTTP 404 if the agent is not found.
Raises HTTP 502 if communication with the orchestrator fails.

parameters.description: The name of the agent whose memory to search.

schema.title: Agent Name
schema.description: The name of the agent whose memory to search.

parameters.name: agent_name

parameters.description: The text query to search for.

schema.title: Query
schema.description: The text query to search for.

parameters.name: query

parameters.description: The maximum number of results to return.

schema.title: Top K
schema.description: The maximum number of results to return.

parameters.name: top_k

schema.title: Response Search Agent Memory Api V1 Memory Agents  Agent Name  Search Get

get.description:
Performs a semantic search across all agent memories (if supported by the orchestrator).

- **query**: The search text.
- **top_k**: Maximum number of results (default 5, max 50).

Sends a `memory_search_global` command to the orchestrator.
Requires an active user session.
Raises HTTP 502 if communication with the orchestrator fails.

parameters.description: The text query to search for.

schema.title: Query
schema.description: The text query to search for.

parameters.name: query

parameters.description: The maximum number of results to return.

schema.title: Top K
schema.description: The maximum number of results to return.

parameters.name: top_k

schema.title: Response Search Global Memory Api V1 Memory Search Get

post.summary: Proxy payload to LM Studio completion endpoint
post.description:
Receives a JSON payload and proxies it directly to the configured LM Studio
chat completions endpoint (e.g., `/v1/chat/completions`).
Returns the raw JSON response from LM Studio.
Proxy a message to the Echo agent via the orchestrator.

schema.title: Response Send Echo Api V1 Echo  Post

get.description: Return system metrics in Prometheus exposition format.
Renders the main feed HTML page.
Returns the latest events from the orchestrator as JSON.
Simple health check endpoint.
